response.justification: Mohammed Al-Said is an experienced web and full-stack engineer with over 15 years in software development, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The position requires native-level Japanese proficiency, which is an absolute disqualifier as the candidate lists only English, Romanian, and basic German. Beyond the language barrier, the role demands deep expertise in embedded C programming, Linux kernel modification, and LAN-switch security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal), none of which appear in the candidate's background. His career is centered on web technologies, TypeScript, Node.js, and cloud infrastructure — a stack entirely orthogonal to embedded firmware development for network switches.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Some C/C++ exposure through National Instruments and a wave field synthesis thesis project using Linux and C++
Strong Linux and DevOps familiarity, including CI/CD pipelines, Docker, and open-source project maintenance
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency — native-level Japanese is a hard requirement for this role
No embedded networking or security experience (MACsec, 802.1X, captive portal, switch hardware, NETCONF, SNMP are entirely absent)
Career trajectory is firmly in web/cloud/TypeScript — C programming and Linux kernel work are peripheral and dated, not a professional focus
